meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop consuming tutorials and start shipping. Learn how to use project management frameworks with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start architecting. By shifting from passive consumption to a project-management-first workflow, you break the cycle. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution. This combination transforms abstract concepts into production-ready code, allowing you to ship features faster than competitors.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'Build a To-Do App' video, but your production environment is empty. You’ve mastered the syntax of ten different frameworks, yet your client portfolio remains stagnant. You are trapped in the endless loop of tutorial hell, where the comfort of guided instruction replaces the grit of actual engineering.

Real-world development isn't about following a script; it's about managing complexity. To break free, you must treat every build as a professional project with a defined scope, architecture, and deployment strategy. By integrating Antigravity’s rapid scaffolding with the relentless execution power of Claude Code, you move from a student of code to a builder of systems.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Before touching code, define exactly what your client needs. Break the project into three core features to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Create a project specification document for a [Project Name] MVP. Define the core entities, database schema, and the top 3 user flows that provide the most value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create project-spec.md based on the defined requirements'

steps.title: Scaffold the Foundation
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. This saves hours of configuration and ensures your project adheres to best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for [Framework/Language] with integrated authentication, database ORM, and API routing. Follow clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure from antigravity boilerplate'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Implement features one by one using Claude Code. Focus on tests first to ensure your code is robust and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the implementation plan for the [Feature Name] module. Include unit test requirements and necessary API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement [Feature Name] including unit tests and error handling'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Before pushing to production, use Claude to audit your codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a senior engineer code review on the latest commit. Highlight security risks, performance issues, and suggest refactoring for readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'review codebase for security and performance improvements'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Setup
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI/CD pipeline to ensure that your code is always in a deployable state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to [Cloud Provider]. Ensure environment variables are handled securely.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate and configure CI/CD pipeline for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always commit after every successful feature implementation; granular history is your best friend when debugging client feedback.
Treat the README as the source of truth; if it isn't documented there, it doesn't exist for the client.
Use Claude Code to write your documentation in parallel with your features to ensure 100% coverage.
Never accept default configurations for databases; always explicitly define your indexes and constraints to save costs at scale.
Automate your feedback loop by having Claude generate a summary of changes for your client after every sprint.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is live, implement a telemetry layer to track user behavior, then iterate based on actual data rather than assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of impact. Your clients are waiting for a developer who actually ships. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. This saves hours of configuration and ensures your project adheres to best practices.
Implement features one by one using Claude Code. Focus on tests first to ensure your code is robust and maintainable.
Before pushing to production, use Claude to audit your codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks.
Connect your repository to a CI/CD pipeline to ensure that your code is always in a deployable state.
